behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: collectors -> report assembler -> summary input payload. A unit test of one function is too low because the bug appears when multiple components are wired. Integration-brake checks: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess is required to detect missing section handoff, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is about "content passed to the summary-generation step" (handoff correctness), which is reproducible with in-process fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/assembly modules with in-process test doubles for the three sources returning distinct sentinel data. Execute report build up to the summary call and assert the summary input includes all three source payloads (not just two).

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module degradation behavior (source adapter error propagation + report composition fallback), so unit is too low. Integration-brake checks: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; an adapter throwing unavailable can reproduce the failure, (b) correctness does not depend on AI/ML output content, (c) no multi-agentic sequence. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text specifies fallback logic ("`data unavailable` note ... rather than aborting"), which is testable via in-process failure injection.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with two source adapters returning data and one adapter returning an unavailable error. Assert the job completes, report contains a `data unavailable` marker for only that section, and other sections are still present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on output content quality of the summary-generation step. A stubbed/mocked summarizer would hide factual distortion failures and always pass deterministic fixtures. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text covers one model judgment stage ("narrative summary accurately represents...") rather than multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed dataset with known key metrics/issues/deploy facts and call the real summary model/service. Evaluate whether required key values are preserved and not contradicted (e.g., rubric/LLM-judge + rule checks) across repeated runs with a pass threshold to account for nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure surface includes real infrastructure: real HTTP/internal endpoints and real email transport/inbox delivery. In-process mocks can hide auth, network, MIME, and provider-delivery failures. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected as minimum because behavior text focuses on end-to-end delivery ("reaches ... inbox correctly"), not on evaluating model output quality; Workflow rejected because no multi-agentic sequence is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in a staging-like environment against real source endpoints and a real test mailbox. Verify the message is delivered to configured recipients with expected subject/body sections and attachments/format.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior crosses real infra boundaries: unreachable delivery service + filesystem persistence + next-run retry semantics. Mocks often miss real write/permission/path/timing/recovery issues and scheduler/run-boundary behavior. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because no AI decision chain is involved; Agentic rejected because correctness is transport/retry persistence behavior, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: In an environment with real filesystem access, force email service unreachable (e.g., network block or endpoint down) and execute run #1; assert fallback file is written. Execute run #2 on the next schedule (or simulated next process invocation) after restoring service; assert queued report is sent and fallback artifact/state is cleared.
